author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XII. 27–36.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After the Lord Jesus Christ, in the words of yesterday’s lesson, had exhorted His servants to follow Him, and had predicted His own passion in this way, that unless a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone; but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit; and also had stirred up those who wished to follow Him to the kingdom of heaven, to hate their life in this world if their thought was to keep it unto life eternal,—He again toned down His own feelings to our infirmity and says, where our lesson to-day commenced, “Now is my soul troubled.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence, Lord, was Thy soul troubled?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had, indeed, said a little before, “He that hateth his life [soul] in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou then love thy life in this world, and is thy soul troubled as the hour approacheth when thou shalt leave this world?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who would dare affirm this of the soul [life] of the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We rather it was whom He transferred unto Himself; He took us into His own person as our Head, and assumed the feelings of His members; and so it was not by any others He was troubled, but, as was said of Him when He raised Lazarus, “He was troubled in Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it behoved the one Mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus, just as He has lifted us up to the heights of heaven, to descend with us also into the lowest depths of suffering.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I hear Him saying a little before, “The hour cometh that the Son of man should be glorified: if a corn of wheat die, it bringeth forth much fruit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I hear this also, “He that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor am I permitted merely to admire, but commanded to imitate, and so, by the words that follow, “If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be,” I am all on fire to despise the world, and in my sight the whole of this life, however lengthened, becomes only a vapor; in comparison with my love for eternal things, all that is temporal has lost its value with me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now, again, it is my Lord Himself, who by such words has suddenly transported me from the weakness that was mine to the strength that was His, that I hear saying, “Now is my soul troubled.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does it mean?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How biddest Thou my soul follow Thee if I behold Thine own troubled?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How shall I endure what is felt to be heavy by strength so great?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the kind of foundation I can seek if the Rock is giving way?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But methinks I hear in my own thoughts the Lord giving me an answer, saying, Thou shalt follow me the better, because it is to aid thy power of endurance that I thus interpose.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast heard, as addressed to thyself, the voice of my fortitude; hear in me the voice of thy infirmity: I supply strength for thy running, and I check not thy hastening, but I transfer to myself thy causes for trembling, and I pave the way for thy marching along.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O Lord our Mediator, God above us, man for us, I own Thy mercy!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For because Thou, who art so great, art troubled through the good will of Thy love, Thou preservest, by the richness of Thy comfort, the many in Thy body who are troubled by the continual experience of their own weakness, from perishing utterly in their despair.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a word, let the man who would follow learn the road by which he must travel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps an hour of terrible trial has come, and the choice is set before thee either to do iniquity or endure suffering; the weak soul is troubled, on whose behalf the invincible soul [of Jesus] was voluntarily troubled; set then the will of God before thine own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For notice what is immediately subjoined by thy Creator and thy Master, by Him who made thee, and became Himself for thy teaching that which He made; for He who made man was made man, but He remained still the unchangeable God, and transplanted manhood into a better condition.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen, then, to what He adds to the words, “Now is my soul troubled.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And what shall I say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Father, save me from this hour: but for this cause came I unto this hour.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Father, glorify Thy name.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has taught thee here what to think of, what to say, on whom to call, in whom to hope, and whose will, as sure and divine, to prefer to thine own, which is human and weak.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Imagine Him not, therefore, as losing aught of His own exalted position in wishing thee to rise up out of the depths of thy ruin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He thought it meet also to be tempted by the devil, by whom otherwise He would never have been tempted, just as, had He not been willing, He would never have suffered; and the answers He gave to the devil are such as thou also oughtest to use in times of temptation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He, indeed, was tempted, but not endangered, that He might show thee, when in danger through temptation, how to answer the tempter, so as not to be carried away by the temptation, but to escape its danger.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when He here said, “Now is my soul troubled;” and also when He says, “My soul is sorrowful, even unto death;” and “Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me;” He assumed the infirmity of man, to teach him, when thereby saddened and troubled, to say what follows: “Nevertheless, Father, not as I will, but as Thou wilt.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thus it is that man is turned from the human to the divine, when the will of God is preferred to his own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But to what do the words “Glorify Thy name” refer, but to His own passion and resurrection?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what else can it mean, but that the Father should thus glorify the Son, who in like manner glorifieth His own name in the similar sufferings of His servants?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence it is recorded of Peter, that for this cause He said concerning him, “Another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not,” because He intended to signify “by what death he should glorify God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore in him, too, did God glorify His name, because thus also does He glorify Christ in His members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then came there a voice from heaven, [saying], I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have both glorified it,” before I created the world, “and I will glorify it again,” when He shall rise from the dead and ascend into heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It may also be otherwise understood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have both glorified it,”—when He was born of the Virgin, when He exercised miraculous powers; when the Magi, guided by a star in the heavens, bowed in adoration before Him; when He was recognized by saints filled with the Holy Spirit; when He was openly proclaimed by the descent of the Spirit in the form of a dove, and pointed out by the voice that sounded from heaven; when He was transfigured on the mount; when He wrought many miracles, cured and cleansed multitudes, fed so vast a number with a very few loaves, commanded the winds and the waves, and raised the dead;—“and I will glorify it again;” when He shall rise from the dead; when death shall have no longer dominion over Him; and when He shall be exalted over the heavens as God, and His glory over all the earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The people therefore that stood by, and heard it, said that it thundered: others said, An angel spake to Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus answered and said, This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He thereby showed that the voice made no intimation to Him of what He already knew, but to those who needed the information.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And just as that voice was uttered by God, not on His account, but on that of others, so His soul was troubled, not on His own account, but voluntarily for the sake of others.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look at what follows: “Now,” He says, “is the judgment of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, are we to expect at the end of time?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the judgment that is looked for in the end will be the judging of the living and the dead, the awarding of eternal rewards and punishment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of what sort, then, is the judgment now?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have already, in former lessons, as far as I could, put you in mind, beloved, that there is a judgment spoken of, not of condemnation, but of discrimination; as it is written, “Judge me, O God, and plead [discern, discriminate] my cause against an unholy nation.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And many are the judgments of God; as it is said in the psalm, “Thy judgments are a great deep.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the apostle also says, “O the depth of the riches of the wisdom and the knowledge of God! how unsearchable are His judgments!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To such judgments does that spoken of here by the Lord also belong, “Now is the judgment of this world;” while that judgment in the end is reserved, when the living and the dead shall at last be judged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The devil, therefore, had possession of the human race, and held them by the written bond of their sins as criminals amenable to punishment; he ruled in the hearts of unbelievers, and, deceiving and enslaving them, seduced them to forsake the Creator and give worship to the creature; but by faith in Christ, which was confirmed by His death and resurrection, and, by His blood, which was shed for the remission of sins, thousands of believers are delivered from the dominion of the devil, are united to the body of Christ, and under this great head are made by His one Spirit to spring up into new life as His faithful members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This it was that He called the judgment, this righteous separation, this expulsion of the devil from His own redeemed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Attend, in short, to His own words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For just as if we had been inquiring what He meant by saying, “Now is the judgment of the world,” He proceeded to explain it when He says, “Now shall the prince of this world be cast out.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What we have thus heard was the kind of judgment He meant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that one, therefore, which is yet to come in the end, when the living and dead shall be judged, some of them set apart on His right hand, and the others on His left; but that judgment by which “the prince of this world shall be cast out.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what sense, then, was he within, and whither did He mean that he was to be cast out?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it this: That he was in the world. and was cast forth beyond its boundaries?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For had He been speaking of that judgment which is yet to come in the end, some one’s thoughts might have turned to that eternal fire into which the devil is to be cast with his angels, and all who belong to him;—that is, not naturally, but through moral delinquency; not because he created or begat them, but because he persuaded and kept hold of them: some one, therefore, might have thought that that eternal fire was outside the world, and that this was the meaning of the words, “he shall be cast out.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as He says, “Now is the judgment of this world,” and in explanation of His meaning, adds, “Now shall the prince of this world be cast out,” we are thereby to understand what is now being done, and not what is to be, so long afterwards, at the last day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, therefore, foretold what He knew, that after His own passion and glorification, many nations throughout the whole world, in whose hearts the devil was an inmate, would become believers, and the devil, when thus renounced by faith, is cast out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But some one says, Was he then not cast out of the hearts of the patriarchs and prophets, and the righteous of olden time?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly he was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, is it said, “Now he shall be cast out”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How else can we think of it, but that what was then done in the case of a very few individuals, was now foretold as speedily to take place in many and mighty nations?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as also that other saying, “For the Spirit was not yet given, because that Jesus was not yet glorified,” may suggest a similar inquiry, and find a similar solution.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was not without the Holy Spirit that the prophets predicted the events of the future; nor was it so that the aged Simeon and the widowed Anna knew by the Holy Spirit the infant Lord; and that Zacharias and Elisabeth uttered by the Holy Spirit so many predictions concerning Him, when He was not yet born, but only conceived.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But “the Spirit was not yet given;” that is, with that abundance of spiritual grace which enabled those assembled together to speak in every language, and thus announce beforehand in the language of every nation the Church of the future: and so by this spiritual grace it was that nations were gathered into congregations, sins were pardoned far and wide, and thousands of thousands were reconciled unto God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But then, says some one, since the devil is thus cast out of the hearts of believers, does he now tempt none of the faithful?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nay, verily, he does not cease to tempt.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it is one thing to reign within, another to assail from without; for in like manner the best fortified city is sometimes attacked by an enemy without being taken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if some of his arrows are discharged, and reach us, the apostle reminds us how to render them harmless, when he speaks of the breastplate and the shield of faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if he sometimes wounds us, we have the remedy at hand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as the combatants are told, “These things I write unto you, that ye sin not:” so those who are wounded have the sequel to listen to, “And if any man sin, we have an Advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ, the righteous; and He is the propitiation for our sins.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what do we pray for when we say, “Forgive us our debts,” but for the healing of our wounds?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what else do we ask, when we say, “Lead us not into temptation,” but that he who thus lies in wait for us, or assails us from without, may fail on every side to effect an entrance, and be unable to overcome us either by fraud or force?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, whatever engines of war he may erect against us, so long as he has no more a place in the heart that faith inhabits, he is cast out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But “except the Lord keep the city, the watchman waketh but in vain.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Presume not, therefore, about yourselves, if you would not have the devil, who has once been cast out, to be recalled within.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On the other hand, let us be far from supposing that the devil is called in any such way the prince of the world, as that we should believe him possessed of power to rule over the heaven and the earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The world is so spoken of in respect of wicked men, who have overspread the whole earth; just as a house is spoken of in respect to its inhabitants, and we accordingly say, It is a good house, or a bad house; not as finding fault with, or approving of, the erection of walls and roofs, but the morals either of the good or the bad within it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a similar way, therefore, it is said, “The prince of this world;” that is, the prince of all the wicked who inhabit this world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The world is also spoken of in respect to the good, who in like manner have overspread the whole earth; and hence the apostle says, “God was in Christ, reconciling the world unto Himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These are they out of whose hearts the prince of this world is ejected.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, after saying, “Now shall the prince of this world be cast out,” He added, “And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all things after me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what “all” is that, but those out of which the other is ejected?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He did not say, All men, but “all things;” for all men have not faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, therefore, He did not allude to the totality of men, but to the creature in its personal integrity, that is, to spirit, and soul, and body; or all that which makes us the intelligent, living, visible, and palpable beings we are.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He who said, “Not a hair of your head shall perish,” is He who draweth all things after Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or if by “all things” it is men that are to be understood, we can speak of all things that are foreordained to salvation: of all which He declared, when previously speaking of His sheep, that not one of them would be lost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And of a certainty all classes of men, both of every language and every age, and all grades of rank, and all diversities of talents, and all the professions of lawful and useful arts, and all else that can be named in accordance with the innumerable differences by which men, save in sin alone, are mutually separated, from the highest to the lowest, and from the king to the beggar, “all,” He says, “will I draw after me;” that He may be their head, and they His members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this will be, He adds, “if I be lifted up from the earth,” that is, when I am lifted up; for He has no doubt of the future accomplishment of that which He came to fulfill.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He here alludes to what He said before: “But if the corn of wheat die, it bringeth forth much fruit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what else did He signify by His lifting up, than His suffering on the cross, an explanation which the evangelist himself has not omitted; for he has appended the words, “And this He said signifying what death He should die.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The people answered Him, We have heard out of the law that Christ abideth for ever: and how sayest Thou, The Son of man must be lifted up?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who is this Son of man?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It had stuck to their memory that the Lord was constantly calling Himself the Son of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, in the passage before us, He does not say, If the Son of man be lifted up from the earth; but had called Himself so before, in the lesson which was read and expounded yesterday, when those Gentiles were announced who desired to see Him: “The hour is come that the Son of man should be glorified” (ver.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Retaining this, therefore, in their minds, and understanding what He now said, “When I am lifted up from the earth,” of the death of the cross, they inquired of Him, and said, “We have heard out of the law that Christ abideth for ever; and how sayest Thou, The Son of man must be lifted up? who is this Son of man?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if it is Christ, He, they say, abideth for ever; and if He abideth for ever, how shall He be lifted up from the earth, that is, how shall He die through the suffering of the cross?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they understood Him to have spoken of what they themselves were meditating to do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so He did not dissipate for them the obscurity of such words by imparting wisdom, but by stimulating their conscience.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then said Jesus unto them, Yet a little light is in you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And by this it is you understand that Christ abideth for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Walk, then, while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Walk, draw near, come to the full understanding that Christ shall both die and shall live for ever; that He shall shed His blood to redeem us, and ascend on high to carry His redeemed along with Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But darkness will come upon you, if your belief in Christ’s eternity is of such a kind as to refuse to admit in His case the humiliation of death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So may he stumble on that stone of stumbling and rock of offence which the Lord Himself became to the blinded Jews: just as to those who believed, the stone which the builders despised was made the head of the corner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, they thought Christ unworthy of their belief; because in their impiety they treated His dying with contempt, they ridiculed the idea of His being slain: and yet it was the very death of the grain of corn that was to lead to its own multiplication, and the lifting up of one who was drawing all things after Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “While ye have the light,” He adds, “believe in the light, that ye may be the children of light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: While you have possession of some truth that you have heard, believe in the truth, that you may be born again in the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “These things spake Jesus, and departed, and did hide Himself from them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not from those who had begun to believe and to love Him, nor from those who had come to meet Him with branches of palm trees and songs of praise; but from those who saw and hated Him, for they saw Him not, but only stumbled on that stone in their blindness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when Jesus hid Himself from those who desired to slay Him (as you need from forgetfulness to be often reminded), He had regard to our human weakness, but derogated not in aught from His own authority.
